summary: Bootstrapped WHOMA MVP foundation (Next.js/Tailwind/Prisma/Zod/tests), built branded UI scaffolds for core homeowner/agent flows, and added in-repo docs operating system (DEVLOG/TASKS/ARCHITECTURE + startup rule).
decisions: Keep MVP domain entities lean (User/Profile/Property/Instruction/Proposal/Thread/Message only)
Encode structured comparable proposal schema early via Zod
Use UI-first scaffolds before persistence/auth wiring
Adopt append-only dev log + machine-readable changelog for agent handoffs
next_steps: Implement auth + RBAC route protection
Persist instruction creation with Prisma and server-side validation
Persist proposal submission and shortlist/award workflow with gated chat unlock

summary: Installed dependencies, fixed a landing-page JSX parse error and Next.js config warning, then started the dev server and verified key routes render locally.
decisions: Leave dev server running for immediate local preview
Fix runtime preview blockers immediately rather than deferring
next_steps: Implement auth + RBAC route protection
Wire Prisma-backed instruction/proposal writes with server-side validation

summary: Implemented lean signed-cookie dev auth with middleware route protection and server-enforced RBAC + Zod validation on instruction/proposal write endpoints.
decisions: Use signed-cookie dev auth as a PR-sized bridge before NextAuth/Clerk integration
Enforce page access in middleware and write authorization in route handlers
next_steps: Persist instructions in Prisma behind POST /api/instructions
Persist proposals in Prisma behind POST /api/proposals
Replace dev auth with production auth provider integration when persistence is in place

summary: Refreshed the homepage with benefit-led UK property copy, trust messaging, compare/split sections, logo subtitle, and subtle CSS-only motion (plus small lint cleanup in input primitives).
decisions: Use CSS-only motion instead of Framer Motion for lean MVP scope
Keep tender mechanics visible but make hero headline outcome-first
next_steps: Proceed with Prisma-backed instruction/proposal persistence
Consider early-access capture flow when ready

summary: Added trust/support pages, an HTML sitemap, and public location-browse pages for LIVE Instructions (plus a shared mock location dataset and homepage footer links).
decisions: Use a single root dynamic route for legal/support pages and the HTML sitemap to keep the PR lean
Reuse LIVE Instruction card/data shape for location browse pages so Whoma stays an instruction marketplace, not a property portal
Ship placeholder legal/support copy with visible legal-review TODO notices instead of over-claiming completeness
next_steps: Implement cookie consent mechanism and connect it to the cookies page
Replace mock location data with Prisma-backed LIVE Instructions after persistence work

summary: Added a dev-only NextAuth preview provider and local preview access buttons so protected homeowner/agent areas can be explored without Google OAuth credentials in local development.
decisions: Keep NextAuth as the single auth system by adding a dev-only Credentials provider instead of reviving custom signed-cookie auth
Show local preview access only in development and only when Google OAuth credentials are missing
next_steps: Use preview buttons to inspect protected homeowner and agent flows locally
Configure Google OAuth env vars to validate real Google sign-in end-to-end

summary: Relaunched the local site preview on port 3012, opened it in-browser, re-authenticated GitHub CLI, created a private GitHub repository for the project, and prepared the repo for first push.
decisions: Create the GitHub repository as private by default while the product evolves
Use localhost port 3012 for local preview continuity
next_steps: Push initial commit to origin/main
Proceed with T002: Prisma-backed homeowner instruction creation

summary: Re-prioritized delivery to Phase 1 real estate agent identity validation, added a living platform architecture map, created a dated Phase 1 execution plan, and updated working rules/tasks to enforce architecture-map updates every session.
decisions: Prioritize real estate agent onboarding/CV/public-profile/directory features before broader homeowner workflow completion
Require architecture-map maintenance on every implementation session
Adopt milestone dates culminating in onboarding pilot start on Monday 2026-03-30
next_steps: Implement A001 onboarding foundation with server-side agent profile bootstrap
Expand AgentProfile schema for structured CV fields and publish controls
Ship public profile and directory routes for pilot onboarding

summary: Implemented Phase 1 real estate agent identity features end-to-end: onboarding flow, CV builder, publishable public profiles, public agent directory, live admin verification queue with funnel counters, and platform-wide copy updates aligned to real estate agent positioning.
decisions: Centralize profile/onboarding logic in a dedicated service layer for future scaling
Require server-side completeness threshold before public profile publishing
Route AGENT users to onboarding by default to enforce Phase 1 activation flow
next_steps: Generate and apply Prisma migrations for new AgentProfile fields and enum
Run full end-to-end QA from onboarding to public directory and admin verification
Start controlled onboarding pilot operations for the Monday 2026-03-30 target

summary: Fixed a dev preview auth regression by adding a DATABASE_URL-aware fallback: preview sign-in now uses Prisma upsert when DB is configured and falls back to stateless preview identities when DB is absent.
decisions: Use dual-mode preview auth to support both DB-backed and DB-less local development
Preserve persistent preview user upserts only when DATABASE_URL exists
next_steps: Configure DATABASE_URL and run migrations to test full persistence paths
Perform DB-backed end-to-end QA for onboarding/profile publish/admin verification

summary: Extended dev-only preview auth to support ADMIN sign-in, preserved DB-aware fallback behavior when DATABASE_URL is missing, and added a Preview as Admin entrypoint that routes to /admin/agents.
decisions: Keep preview auth development-only so production auth behavior stays unchanged
Preserve stateless preview fallback when DATABASE_URL is absent
Route the admin preview button directly to /admin/agents for QA
next_steps: Use the new admin preview path for QA on /admin/agents
Continue with the next PR-sized marketplace or persistence slice

summary: Applied the Phase 1 Prisma migration to local Postgres, added DB-backed Phase 1 service/E2E tests, fixed publish redirect behavior, and hardened profile publish/verification reliability for pilot onboarding.
decisions: Use checked-in migration SQL as the reliable local execution path when Prisma schema engine commands are unstable in sandbox
Require published/completeness prerequisites before allowing admin verification to keep trust badges meaningful
Keep both DB-backed service tests and E2E coverage for Phase 1 onboarding -> publish -> verification transitions
next_steps: Run pilot onboarding against the DB-backed flow ahead of Monday 2026-03-30
Begin Phase 2 persistence work (T002/T003) on top of the stabilized Phase 1 foundation

summary: Implemented a marketplace write infrastructure baseline: Prisma-backed instruction/proposal persistence with service-layer domain guards, typed API error semantics, health endpoint DB readiness checks, and structured instruction must-have persistence.
decisions: Create a dedicated marketplace service layer before UI wiring so domain invariants stay centralized and reusable
Use typed service error codes mapped to HTTP status codes for deterministic operational handling
Persist Instruction.mustHaves as structured array data now to keep seller requirements queryable
Gate DB integration tests behind RUN_DB_TESTS=true to keep default test runs deterministic when Postgres is offline
Capture a dedicated backend scale blueprint so future coding agents can execute against explicit infrastructure contracts
next_steps: Wire homeowner and agent proposal forms to the persisted write APIs with user-visible success/error states
Replace mock marketplace listing data with Prisma-backed LIVE instruction queries
Add idempotency keys and rate limiting on write endpoints before broader external rollout

summary: Converted /homeowner/instructions/new from scaffold UI into a working client-side instruction form with controlled inputs, bid-window synchronization, structured must-have parsing, idempotent POST submission, and envelope-based success/error states.
decisions: Keep the form logic self-contained in the page file to stay within the requested ownership scope
Synchronize bid window values client-side so the posted payload stays aligned with the server schema
Show API envelope errors directly in the page so homeowners get immediate feedback on submission issues
next_steps: Wire the homeowner proposal compare/shortlist view to persisted proposal data
Add client-side schema parity if another form-focused slice is needed

summary: Packaged the Phase 1 DB-backed hardening release with idempotent pilot-agent seeding and a single weekly demo automation command covering seed + DB service test + E2E onboarding/publish/verification flow.
decisions: Use idempotent upserts for pilot seeding so weekly reruns do not duplicate records
Bundle seed + DB-backed service test + Playwright E2E into one weekly run command for reliable meeting prep
Seed mixed profile and verification states to demonstrate realistic funnel and admin verification behavior
next_steps: Adopt npm run demo:phase1:weekly as standard weekly pre-demo runbook
Proceed to next marketplace persistence/UI slices while preserving Phase 1 reliability baseline

summary: Replaced mock marketplace and location browse reads with Prisma-backed LIVE instruction queries, added database proposal counts and postcode-district grouping, and preserved usable empty states when DATABASE_URL is missing.
decisions: Centralize marketplace reads in a dedicated server module so the card shape stays consistent across surfaces
Render the pages dynamically so they always reflect live bid-window state
Return graceful empty states when the database is not configured instead of crashing
next_steps: Add focused tests for the new read helpers and empty-state handling
Route any remaining marketplace read surfaces through the same live query module

summary: Wired the agent proposal builder into a live client-side submit flow with inline validation, live preview sync, and structured POST /api/proposals submissions using idempotency headers.
decisions: Keep the route wrapper server-rendered and move interactivity into a local client component
Reuse the shared proposal submission schema for browser-side validation
Leave draft/chat secondary actions unwired in this PR-sized slice
next_steps: Complete the rest of T010 only if the product wants RHF-based field registration
Proceed with homeowner compare/shortlist/award workflow after proposal submit wiring is stable

summary: Integrated rollout complete: homeowner and agent forms now submit to persisted APIs, marketplace/location reads use Prisma LIVE data, and write endpoints enforce idempotency + rate limiting for safer web rollout.
decisions: Use route handlers as API boundaries and centralize write/read domain logic in server modules
Require Idempotency-Key on write endpoints and persist replay metadata in the database
Apply actor-scoped in-memory rate limiting as immediate rollout protection without new infrastructure dependencies
Render marketplace/location empty states when DATABASE_URL is absent so pages remain stable in dev and partial infra environments
next_steps: Implement T004 compare/shortlist/award persistence on top of the new contracts
Add durable audit/event persistence for write outcomes and workflow transitions
Move rate limiting to a shared distributed backend before multi-instance production rollout

summary: Completed stabilization sprint to break endpoint-debug loops: enforced canonical dev host behavior, added hydration-ready form guardrails, created a repeatable marketplace smoke gate command, and documented deterministic migration recovery workflow.
decisions: Redirect dev traffic to canonical AUTH_URL host in middleware to avoid localhost/127 cookie and callback mismatches
Expose data-form-ready hydration markers and disable form controls until interactive to reduce first-load flake
Add one deterministic smoke gate command for homeowner-create + agent-submit workflow validation
Document migration fallback and reconcile workflow in a dedicated runbook
next_steps: Implement T004 compare/shortlist/award persistence on top of stabilized auth and form interactivity
Consider wiring smoke gate into CI pre-merge checks
Move rate limits from in-memory to shared backing store before multi-instance production rollout

summary: Completed T004 with persisted homeowner compare data and decision writes: shortlist/reject/award now run through a guarded decision API with idempotency, rate limiting, transactional transition rules, and award exclusivity.
decisions: Keep decision invariants in marketplace service layer and keep the route as a boundary adapter
Apply the same idempotency and actor-scoped rate-limit pattern used by other marketplace write endpoints
Use optimistic compare UI updates with rollback so UX stays responsive while server remains source of truth
next_steps: Implement T005 message-thread unlock rules driven by shortlist/award state transitions
Add homeowner compare decision e2e coverage for shortlist/reject/award interactions
Move in-memory rate limiting toward shared distributed infrastructure before multi-instance production traffic

summary: Hardened Phase 1 trust continuity by requiring re-verification on material profile edits, gating CV builder access behind completed onboarding, simplifying verified-only public directory semantics, and reconciling architecture/task docs to current behavior.
decisions: Reset verificationStatus to PENDING when a previously verified profile is materially edited so trust badges stay accurate over time
Require completed onboarding before agents can access the CV builder to prevent guided-flow bypasses
Remove the redundant verified checkbox from public directory UI because exposure is intentionally fixed to VERIFIED + PUBLISHED agents
next_steps: Implement A010 production email delivery and verification abuse controls (resend cooldowns and attempt limits)
Add deterministic local server readiness checks before Phase 1 Playwright runs to reduce QA flake
Package and push this trust-hardening/docs-alignment slice as the next clean commit

summary: Implemented T005 persistence progress by creating LOCKED message threads on proposal submit and unlocking OPEN on shortlist/award decisions, plus added homeowner compare decision E2E coverage with host-canonicalization-safe sign-in and persisted status assertions.
decisions: Enforce message-thread lock/open transitions inside existing marketplace transactions to prevent decision/thread state drift
Align E2E execution to canonical auth host behavior (localhost vs 127.0.0.1) to avoid session cookie split-brain failures
Keep T005 open until message API read/write authorization guarantees are implemented and tested
next_steps: Implement API-level message read/write authorization for homeowner+relevant-agent only
Ship shared-store rate limiting/idempotency hardening (Redis/Upstash) for multi-instance scale
Add durable workflow audit/outbox events for proposal decisions and thread unlock transitions

summary: Completed T005 API authorization by adding participant-scoped message thread read/write endpoints with RBAC, rate limiting, idempotent writes, and locked-thread enforcement on top of existing lock/unlock persistence.
decisions: Enforce participant checks in service helpers and keep API routes as boundary adapters for consistent authorization behavior
Mask outsider thread access as not-found to prevent thread existence leakage
Require idempotency keys for message writes to align with existing marketplace write safety policy
next_steps: Wire the /messages UI to the new message APIs and add a thread list query endpoint for thread discovery
Move rate limiting and idempotency backing from in-memory storage to shared Redis/Upstash infrastructure
Add durable audit/outbox events for shortlist, award, unlock, and message write actions

summary: Wired /messages to live persisted APIs (thread list/read/send) and upgraded rate-limit/idempotency storage to optional shared Upstash Redis with safe fallback for multi-instance readiness.
decisions: Added a dedicated /api/messages/threads read endpoint to support discoverable thread listing in /messages
Implemented Upstash REST-backed shared storage for rate limiting and idempotency while preserving local fallback behavior
Deep-linked compare CTA to /messages?instructionId=... so shortlist/award workflows hand off directly into relevant messaging context
next_steps: Add unread/read-state policy and lightweight revalidation for near-real-time message updates
Add durable outbox/audit events for message sends and unlock transitions
Define environment policy for requiring Upstash in non-development deployments

summary: Deployed WHOMA live on Railway with managed Postgres, startup Prisma migrations, production env wiring, auth/session hardening fixes, and verified end-to-end smoke flow on the public URL.
decisions: Use Railway for immediate managed app+Postgres deployment and shareable URL in one session
Enable preview auth in production only via explicit env gate for staging/demo usability
Run prisma migrate deploy at startup to keep runtime schema aligned
Temporarily set next.config typescript.ignoreBuildErrors to bypass known baseline type debt and unblock deployment
next_steps: Configure production Google OAuth credentials and disable preview fallback in production
Remove ignoreBuildErrors after repository-wide type debt cleanup
Complete A010 production work-email delivery and anti-abuse controls

summary: Implemented A010 anti-abuse controls (resend cooldown, verify-attempt lockout, onboarding rate limits) with Resend delivery adapter wiring, removed next.config ignoreBuildErrors after strict type/build cleanup, and redeployed Railway with passing live health/smoke verification.
decisions: Enforce work-email verification anti-abuse at the service boundary (cooldown + attempt lockout) and add action-layer rate limits for defense in depth.
Restore strict production compile gates by removing typescript.ignoreBuildErrors after targeted type-system cleanup.
Keep preview auth enabled in production temporarily until Google OAuth credentials are provisioned to avoid accidental auth outage during transition.
next_steps: Set Railway production credentials: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_FROM_EMAIL.
Set ENABLE_PREVIEW_AUTH=false after Google OAuth credentials are verified in production.
Run a production onboarding verification email send/confirm check to close A010 acceptance fully.

summary: Reworked preview auth to support personal email entry + compact role selector, fixed sign-in preview button UX overflow, updated smoke script selectors, and redeployed/verified live preview sign-in flow.
decisions: Use personal-email preview fallback for immediate access while Google OAuth remains unconfigured.
Replace long role-labeled preview buttons with compact role selectors plus a single submit CTA to prevent UI overlap.
Update smoke script to support both legacy and new preview selectors to preserve deployment verification.
next_steps: Continue using preview email flow for demos while auth credentials are pending.
Provision Google OAuth credentials and disable preview fallback when ready for stricter production posture.
Add a dedicated UI test for preview-email auth interactions.

summary: Rewrote public-facing copy on landing, auth, directory, location browse, and static policy pages to use outcome-first language and clearer empty states without changing routes or data wiring.
decisions: Keep layout and routing untouched while rewriting only public copy
Use brief / response window / responses language on public surfaces to reduce internal jargon
next_steps: If desired, apply the same wording cleanup to the remaining public-facing marketplace screens outside this file set
Consider a broader copy consistency pass for any remaining public labels that still mention internal workflow terms

summary: Implemented A012 AI resume intake for agent onboarding with OCR + LLM hybrid extraction, rollout flags, v2 signed suggestion cookie contract, API route handlers, onboarding wiring, and passing targeted tests.
decisions: Kept resume suggestions in signed cookie storage for this phase to preserve suggest-only behavior and avoid premature DB expansion.
Used heuristic-first extraction with optional LLM/OCR augmentation and confidence thresholding to balance quality, latency, and cost.
Maintained existing API conventions (ok/data and ok/error envelopes, idempotency, and rate-limit headers) for consistency.
next_steps: Roll out in production by stages using flags: heuristic-only, shadow mode, partial hybrid, then full hybrid.
Track extraction coverage and latency on benchmark resumes before widening rollout.
Configure OCR provider envs in production if scanned-PDF coverage is needed immediately.

summary: Implemented outcome-first public UX/copy alignment across homepage, auth, agent directory, location feeds, footer, and trust pages; deployed to Railway production and verified health plus smoke flow.
decisions: Applied GPT-5.4 recommendation focus to public-facing trust and clarity first, keeping route/data structure stable for low-risk deployment.
Adopted consumer-facing language on public pages (`sale request`, `offer`, `choose`) while preserving internal workflow domain terms in backend models.
Validated deployment with both health endpoint checks and live marketplace smoke script before closing session.
next_steps: Extend the same language and UX system to logged-in compare/marketplace screens for end-to-end consistency.
Add richer proof blocks to public agent pages (recent local activity / response quality) once supporting fields are available.
Address remaining lint warning in resume suggestion cookie module.

summary: Completed logged-in homeowner compare decision UX (sort modes, badges, shortlist helper), made /requests the primary public seller-request IA with /locations compatibility redirects, deepened /agents/[slug] trust modules, and redeployed/verified Railway live.
decisions: Kept /locations and /locations/[postcodeDistrict] as compatibility redirects to avoid breaking existing shared links while moving public IA to /requests.
Used only existing profile fields for deeper agent proof modules to avoid fabricated trust metrics and keep this release schema-safe.
Upgraded compare UX ergonomics without changing server-side decision transition rules to preserve workflow correctness.
next_steps: Add focused E2E assertions for compare sort/badge/shortlist interactions and decision-highlight rendering.
Monitor /locations redirect usage and remove legacy routes only after external link traffic has migrated.
Address the existing lint warning in src/server/agent-profile/resume-suggestions-cookie.ts.

summary: Repositioned public WHOMA surfaces around verified estate agent identity, hid public preview/admin auth, added shared activation checklist + richer admin metrics, strengthened seeded proof, and verified typecheck/unit/browser coverage.
decisions: Kept preview auth in the backend for QA/E2E but removed all public preview-role UI to restore public trust without breaking test infrastructure.
Reframed /requests* as a noindex secondary pilot instead of deleting it so Phase 2 infrastructure and demos stay intact.
Restricted new activation metrics and checklist states to behaviors already modeled in AgentProfile, avoiding premature transaction/collaboration schema work.
next_steps: Set production company/support env values before a broader public push.
Decide whether /requests* should remain visible as a secondary pilot or become fully hidden once more agent proof exists.
Add durable event persistence if activation metrics need historical reporting instead of query-time counts.

summary: Redeployed Railway production for the Phase 1 public repositioning release, updated the marketplace smoke script to use backend preview callback auth, and re-verified live health, public positioning, pilot/noindex behavior, and end-to-end marketplace smoke flow.
decisions: Kept preview auth backend-only for QA and moved the smoke script to direct callback auth instead of relying on any public preview buttons.
Verified the live release with both public-page checks and a real persisted write-path smoke run so positioning changes and workflow integrity were both covered.
next_steps: Push the release branch to GitHub and open a PR for the deployed Phase 1 repositioning work.
Merge to main when the branch-level release record is approved.
Set production Google/Resend credentials when ready to replace the beta-gated auth path with full public sign-in.

summary: Shifted the public site toward proof-led identity/reputation positioning, strengthened trust/support pages, removed the public auth loading dead end, and upgraded public zero states with clearer pilot-stage guidance.
decisions: Kept the NextAuth backend architecture intact and fixed auth completion by passing public `next`/`error` values from the server into the existing button component.
Held homeowner-facing collaboration behind explicitly labeled pilot CTAs so the public brand remains agent-identity-first.
Mixed live proof (featured verified profile) with sample proof (case study and workflow demo) to improve credibility without inventing unsupported metrics.
next_steps: Replace the sample homepage case study with a live pilot story once enough verified activity exists to do that truthfully.
Set final production company/support env values before widening public distribution beyond the pilot.
Apply the same finished-state/error-state standard to remaining logged-in flows beyond public auth.

summary: Completed remaining Phase 1 product hardening gaps: real logged-in lifecycle dashboards, signed cookie-consent controls, trust-signal fallback labeling on public profiles, auth-entry cleanup, and expanded E2E specs for compare/redirect/trust paths.
decisions: Replaced placeholder logged-in pages with DB-backed lifecycle views instead of adding more static copy so Phase 1 behavior can be measured in-product.
Implemented cookie consent as signed cookie + API controls (no DB migration) to satisfy MVP trust/compliance needs with low rollout risk.
Introduced clearly labeled measured/estimated/unavailable trust signals so sparse pilot data no longer renders empty trust cards.
next_steps: Run updated Playwright suites in CI or unrestricted local runtime (sandbox blocks local server bind on 127.0.0.1:3012).
Set production Google and Resend credentials and decide strict auth posture (`ENABLE_PREVIEW_AUTH=false`) for public production sign-in.
Backfill true measured trust pipelines so estimated trust signals can be phased down.

summary: Redeployed Railway production after lifecycle/consent/trust updates and verified live health, /locations compatibility redirect, cookies consent controls, and end-to-end marketplace smoke write flow.
decisions: Validated production with both route-level checks and a full persisted smoke write path to confirm deployment integrity.
Kept deployment verification aligned to backend preview callback flow so QA smoke remains reliable while public auth stays beta-gated.
next_steps: Run updated Playwright specs in CI or unrestricted runtime for full runtime UX evidence.
Configure production Google and Resend credentials to close strict-auth and A010 verification-delivery readiness.

summary: Ran a structured behavioural-validation audit across narrative, activation, measurement, and trust domains; verified that identity-first positioning is ahead of measurement truth; and added explicit remediation tasks for measurement, transaction logging, narrative truthfulness, and auth posture hardening.
decisions: Treat Phase 1 measurement integrity as a core product requirement rather than a later analytics concern.
Treat backend preview auth posture as a trust blocker until production access is explicitly hardened or disabled.
Record the audit findings directly in repo docs so the next execution pass starts from the same source-of-truth constraints.
next_steps: Fix public narrative truthfulness and production auth posture if backend preview auth is still reachable on the live service.
Add a durable event spine plus explicit behavioural-validation metric definitions and source exclusion.
Model historic and live transaction logging explicitly before expanding public proof claims.

summary: Executed the first Gate 1 trust pass: hard-disabled preview auth in production code, introduced user data-origin tagging with backfill, excluded synthetic actors from production proof/reporting reads, removed unsupported public trust counters, and retired the scaffolded marketplace detail route.
decisions: Closed the production preview-auth hole in code immediately rather than preserving a permissive production-like QA path.
Moved synthetic-actor isolation into first-party schema metadata (`User.dataOrigin`) so public proof/reporting can exclude preview, seed, and test users structurally.
Removed unsupported heuristic and synthetic proof from public pages instead of relabeling it as if that would make it trustworthy.
next_steps: Redeploy and verify live public production no longer exposes the preview provider or accepts preview callback auth.
Complete Gate 1 sign-off with one unrestricted browser-level verification pass against the cleaned public trust surfaces.
Only after Gate 1 and Gate 2 are signed off, begin the explicit Phase 1 measurement contract work.

summary: Shipped the first public brand reset across homepage, shared public scaffolding, entry journeys, and homeowner-pilot surfaces so WHOMA reads as a warmer, profile-first professional product for independent estate agents.
decisions: Moved the homepage from a card-heavy pilot explainer to a profile-led editorial narrative so the first viewport immediately signals a premium agent product.
Updated shared public strings, footer, and logo scaffolding in the same pass so old internal thesis language could not leak back into the header/footer.
Kept homeowner request routes live but demoted their language so they now read as a secondary invited collaboration layer rather than the main category story.
next_steps: Review the new public homepage and request surfaces in-browser on desktop and mobile for final spacing and image-treatment adjustments.
Carry the same brand system through any remaining public profile/detail pages that still feel utility-first.
Decide whether authenticated app chrome should inherit more of the new visual language in a follow-up pass.

summary: Implemented the next launch-readiness pass across signed-in agent/homeowner surfaces, request-area language, system states, and verification plumbing so WHOMA reads more like a finished high-trust product and less like an internal marketplace build.
decisions: Shifted signed-in UI vocabulary to `instruction` and `offer` rather than leaving old marketplace/proposal language in place.
Removed disabled future-feature controls from the offer builder instead of keeping visibly unfinished CTAs in the primary path.
Reconciled Prisma migration history non-destructively so the agent onboarding route could render again without resetting local data.
Aligned Playwright auth verification to a single localhost origin because Auth.js session cookies were being lost across localhost/127.0.0.1 splits.
next_steps: Finish a clean standalone verification of the long Phase 1 agent flow after the auth-origin and migration fixes.
Continue the launch-audit implementation into any remaining public/system surfaces still carrying pre-launch wording.
Run a broader browser review once the last long-form agent E2E path is fully green.

summary: Finished the final local Phase 1 agent-flow verification, fixed the last production build blocker, redeployed Railway production, and verified live health plus hardened invitation-only auth behavior on the public WHOMA URL.
decisions: Fixed the production lint/build blocker locally before deployment so the Railway release path stayed deterministic.
Treated remote preview-auth smoke refusal as the expected production trust posture rather than overriding it for convenience.
Verified live auth hardening with direct health, providers, sign-in, public-route, and callback probes instead of depending on hidden preview UI.
next_steps: Replace or supplement the current preview-auth smoke script with a production-safe live verification path.
Continue the remaining launch-audit implementation across surfaces that still need copy or visual polish.

summary: Deployed the Gate 1 trust release to Railway production from a clean release bundle, fixed the build-blocking legal-page lint issue in that bundle, and verified live provider lockdown, preview callback rejection, scaffold-route retirement, and production data absence for seeded pilot profiles.
decisions: Used a clean temporary deploy bundle built from branch HEAD so unrelated dirty worktree changes from parallel streams would not ship accidentally.
Verified the production auth-backdoor closure with both provider discovery (`/api/auth/providers -> {}`) and failed callback session creation (`error=Configuration`, `/api/auth/session -> null`).
Verified the seeded-profile concern directly in Railway Postgres instead of relying only on page behaviour.
next_steps: Start Gate 2 truth cleanup: CTA routing, public proof labels, /requests public-card split, and docs/tests drift.
Do not begin Gate 3 measurement work until Gate 2 is signed off.

summary: Restored the original Montserrat font family, returned the site-wide background to pure white, updated the stacked WHOMA subtitle to `Where homeowners meet estate agents`, and redeployed Railway production with live verification.
decisions: Applied the font and background change globally so the requested visual baseline stayed consistent across every page.
Normalized the requested subtitle to `Where homeowners meet estate agents` so the live brand line stayed clear and UK-consistent.
Hardcoded the shared subtitle baseline in public-site config for this pass to avoid environment drift on production.
next_steps: Run a small public-surface consistency pass on any remaining routes that can still reintroduce beta or utilitarian tone.
Decide whether the current white-base + Montserrat treatment is the stable visual baseline for future public work.

summary: Closed the remaining BV004 public-truthfulness gap by splitting public request cards from agent-marketplace actions, tightening seller-access route copy, removing the last shared transaction-depth claim, and adding an E2E guard against marketplace links on redirected public routes.
decisions: Used a public-mode variant on the shared instruction card instead of creating a duplicate component so the trust distinction stays explicit and maintainable.
Kept /requests* live as invited seller-access context while removing all agent-marketplace actions from the public surface.
Added a no-marketplace-link browser assertion to the public redirect test so this exact CTA leak cannot quietly return.
next_steps: Design a production-safe live verification path that does not depend on preview callback auth.
Choose whether the next workstream should be Phase 1 measurement primitives (BV001-BV003) or A010 production email delivery.

summary: Unblocked estate-agent self-serve auth by adding DB-backed email/password registration and sign-in, wiring Apple alongside Google in Auth.js when configured, updating public auth pages to show real account-access methods, and removing the end-user-facing service-provider grid from trust pages.
decisions: Added email/password auth instead of waiting on third-party OAuth credentials so estate agents have a real self-serve path immediately.
Kept Google and Apple in the provider stack, but only expose them when live env configuration is present to avoid broken public buttons.
Removed the public service-provider grid from contact/trust pages because it was infra-facing and confusing for end users.
next_steps: Deploy the auth release from a clean scoped commit and verify the live sign-in/sign-up routes.
Add Google and Apple provider secrets in Railway if those OAuth buttons should appear immediately in production.
Choose the long-term managed Postgres strategy for scale beyond the current baseline.

summary: Unified WHOMA's public visual system to the requested clean style by introducing a shared public header across routes, removing fragmented page-level header implementations, and refreshing global tokens/component chrome to eliminate beige/editorial fallback styling.
decisions: Introduced one shared `PublicHeader` instead of continuing per-route header variants so public navigation and CTA hierarchy stay consistent site-wide.
Shifted the style baseline through global tokens and shared primitives first (rather than one-off page tweaks) to prevent warm/beige drift from reappearing.
Kept verification scoped to build/lint plus existing public-routing E2E because the change was visual-system-heavy but still needed route safety coverage.
next_steps: Run a manual desktop/mobile visual sweep on local and preview URLs to confirm spacing and hierarchy after the shared-header rollout.
Consider adding visual regression snapshots for core public pages if style drift continues to recur.

summary: Removed business-domain email gating from agent onboarding/profile flows, stripped response-window/internal operating language from public support/contact surfaces, updated the site-wide brand line to `Where Home Owners Meet Agents`, and reinforced Phase 1 trust proof with inhabited sample-profile content plus explicit sign-in provider-readiness messaging.
decisions: Kept email verification in the activation model but removed business-domain enforcement so onboarding friction does not block valid agents.
Replaced internal support/operations wording on public pages with user-facing access/support language to keep the product narrative externally credible.
Added inhabited sample proof blocks and explicit OAuth readiness copy so early visitors understand both platform direction and current auth-provider availability.
next_steps: Configure live Google/Apple provider credentials in production if those sign-in options should be visible immediately.
Continue tightening Phase 1 public narrative so requests/comparison surfaces remain clearly secondary until Phase 2 thresholds are met.

summary: Delivered a production-ready public auth pass: method-first sign-in UX (Google, Apple, email magic link), post-auth access-state routing (`APPROVED`/`PENDING`/`DENIED`), dedicated pending/denied pages, and explicit denied-state support via `VerificationStatus.REJECTED`.
decisions: Kept the existing `next-auth` stack and extended it with Email magic-link delivery via Resend instead of replacing auth infrastructure.
Separated authentication from approval by introducing explicit post-auth access-state routing rather than blocking sign-in.
Kept request-access/support as a secondary path so public auth remains product-led, not support-led.
next_steps: Deploy this exact commit to Railway and verify live sign-in/callback/pending/denied behavior.
Configure live Google and Apple secrets in production where needed so both OAuth methods are immediately available.

summary: Removed remaining internal-facing language from homepage/contact/onboarding surfaces by deleting the `Evidence signal` block, replacing conditional auth wording on contact, and refining onboarding/profile progress copy to read premium and user-facing.
decisions: Removed the homepage strategy banner entirely instead of rewriting it because the requirement was explicit: no internal language on forward-facing pages.
Kept the progress/tracker product surfaces but rewrote labels and helper copy so they remain useful without sounding like internal operating dashboards.
Verified live copy with cache-bypassed URL checks after Railway deploy to avoid stale edge-cache false positives.
next_steps: Do one manual screenshot QA pass across `/`, `/contact`, and signed-in `/agent/onboarding` to confirm visual tone matches the latest reference set.
If needed, continue softening roadmap wording (`liquidity`, `proof`) on the homepage strip in a dedicated copy pass.

summary: Implemented a production-ready FAQ IA layer: added a thin top utility strip above main public nav, shipped grouped `/faqs`, added homepage FAQ preview, and consolidated support/FAQ links across footer, contact, and sitemap surfaces.
decisions: Centralized FAQ copy and topic structure in one shared source (`src/lib/faqs.ts`) to prevent drift between `/faqs`, homepage preview, and contact quick FAQ content.
Implemented the utility strip in the shared `PublicHeader` component to keep behavior site-wide and avoid route-level duplication.
Used semantic `<details>` blocks for FAQ interaction to keep the implementation lightweight, accessible, and easy to scan without extra client-side JS.
next_steps: Run a quick manual visual sweep on desktop/mobile to validate utility-strip wrapping and FAQ spacing.
Optionally add FAQ route coverage to public routing E2E if regression frequency on public IA increases.

summary: Rewrote all public FAQ answers to a warmer, clearer, and more approachable tone while keeping structure and product-truth positioning intact.
decisions: Updated answers across every FAQ category without changing question titles or IA structure to preserve scanability.
Prioritized direct, inviting language over abstract framing while keeping agent-first and selective-access truth constraints.
Scoped changes to shared FAQ copy source so homepage/contact previews inherit the same improved tone automatically.
next_steps: Deploy to Railway and verify `/faqs` copy on the live URL.
Collect one user-style review pass for any remaining phrases that feel too formal.

summary: Hardened FAQ freshness by making `/faqs` dynamic to avoid stale long-lived edge-cached HTML after copy updates; also refined the FAQ intro line to be friendlier.
decisions: Set `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` on `/faqs` to ensure trust-critical copy changes are visible immediately after deploy.
Kept FAQ IA/content model unchanged in this patch to scope strictly to freshness and tone of the page intro.
next_steps: Deploy and verify `/faqs` on production reflects the latest copy without stale content.
If needed, apply the same cache strategy to other copy-sensitive static trust pages.

summary: Refactored agent onboarding from form-first to AI-assisted profile generation with a dominant upload entry, generated draft preview, gap-focused completion panel, and milestone-oriented progress framing while preserving server-side onboarding/verification rules.
decisions: Kept onboarding and email-verification server actions unchanged to avoid domain-policy regressions while improving UX choreography.
Promoted resume upload as the singular hero action and demoted manual form entry to a secondary confirmation step.
Demoted verification UI to a compact publish gate while retaining the same backend requirement before onboarding completion/publish.
next_steps: Run a manual desktop/mobile UX pass on /agent/onboarding to validate hierarchy and readability in real browser conditions.
Add Playwright coverage for upload-first onboarding flow, low-confidence guidance visibility, and publish-gate behavior.

summary: Migrated production auth from NextAuth to Supabase Auth using Google OAuth + email magic links only, removed Apple and email/password flows, and preserved post-auth role/access-state gating for existing server routes.
decisions: Kept existing app-side auth call sites stable by implementing a Supabase-backed `auth()` compatibility layer instead of refactoring every route/page immediately.
Removed Apple and email/password auth entirely to match product direction and reduce failure modes during production rollout.
Kept account approval logic after authentication (`APPROVED/PENDING/DENIED`) so authentication remains independent from access review.
next_steps: Configure Supabase project auth settings and Google OAuth credentials for Railway production callback URLs.
Run live production auth QA: Google sign-in, email magic-link sign-in, callback error handling, and pending/denied route outcomes.

summary: Completed AI-first agent onboarding delivery: upload-dominant UX, pasted-bio extraction path, GPT-5.4/GPT-5.4-mini extraction defaults, confidence-aware completion framing, and dedicated desktop/mobile onboarding UX Playwright coverage.
decisions: Kept onboarding writes and verification enforcement in existing server actions while redesigning the UX around extraction-first profile generation to minimize backend risk.
Added pasted-bio as a first-class extraction input through the same pipeline so agents can start without a file upload when needed.
Aligned resume-AI defaults to GPT-first operation (gpt-5.4 extraction + gpt-5.4-mini cleanup) with optional OCR fallback disabled by default.
next_steps: Deploy the current branch to Railway production service (`whoma-web`) and verify `/api/health` plus `/agent/onboarding` route behavior.
Run one post-deploy authenticated sanity pass to confirm CV upload, pasted-bio extraction, and publish-gate verification prompts on the live app.

summary: Deployed AI-first onboarding release commit `fed36df` to Railway production (`whoma-web`) and verified live health plus protected onboarding route gating.
decisions: Kept deployment in CI log-stream mode (`railway up --ci`) to capture deterministic build output and completion status.
Verified route-level production behavior immediately after deploy using explicit health and auth-gate checks on the live Railway URL.
next_steps: Run an authenticated production QA pass to confirm CV upload and pasted-bio extraction interactions under real session conditions.

summary: Hardened public Phase 1 narrative around measurable proof-loop execution by adding a homepage validation dashboard/checklist, de-emphasizing future-state shortlist storytelling, and aligning trust/sign-in copy to current product truth.
decisions: Used the existing Phase 1 objective snapshot service as the homepage metrics source of truth instead of introducing a duplicate public-metrics implementation.
Kept future homeowner shortlist storytelling behind an explicit optional preview path and marked examples as illustrative to avoid Phase 2 overshadowing.
Mapped public trust language to measurable behavioural signals and removed remaining internal/ambiguous labels from public-facing surfaces.
next_steps: Run the onboarding E2E suite in the intended auth harness (or update auth test harness to current Supabase callback flow) to restore green onboarding browser automation.
Deploy the branch to production and verify homepage/profile/directory copy and visibility behavior on the live Railway URL.

summary: Finalized the Phase 1 public validation pass by hardening objective-driven homepage messaging, de-emphasizing Phase 2 visuals via feature gating, aligning verification/sign-in copy, and stabilizing related Playwright coverage with environment-aware auth guards.
decisions: Kept Phase 1 strategy visible through explicit objective/target/status UI rather than profile-led narrative alone.
Defaulted `NEXT_PUBLIC_SHOW_PHASE2_PREVIEW` to `false` so future homeowner shortlist flow does not dominate current Phase 1 positioning.
Added preview-auth availability guards in E2E onboarding flows so non-auth environments skip safely instead of failing unrelated release checks.
next_steps: Deploy current branch to Railway production and verify the public homepage/agents/contact surfaces.
Run authenticated production sanity checks for onboarding and publish-gate behaviour once QA credentials are available.

summary: Deployed commit `11864a4` to Railway production service `whoma-web` and verified live health/auth-gate behaviour after the Phase 1 validation dashboard release.
decisions: Used `npx @railway/cli up --ci` to avoid local global-CLI dependency and preserve reproducible deployment output.
Validated deployment success with explicit health and protected-route checks immediately post-release.
next_steps: Run one authenticated production sanity pass for `/agent/onboarding` upload-first and publish-gate flow.
Monitor objective counts on the new homepage validation dashboard as production event volume grows.

summary: Implemented a safe-branch public proof-ledger slice on `/agents/[slug]` using existing durable ProductEvent history, with explicit logged-vs-verified milestone labels and no schema migration.
decisions: Reused durable ProductEvent data for public proof ledger rows to keep the change low risk and migration-free.
Added explicit row-level status language (`Logged signal` and `Verified milestone`) to prevent overclaiming verification depth.
Scoped this slice to read-only public proof presentation, deferring first-class transaction evidence entities to the BV003 follow-on.
next_steps: Run the full preview-auth-enabled phase1 Playwright flow so the auth-dependent spec executes instead of skipping in fallback harnesses.
Deploy this branch once approved and verify live `/agents/[slug]` proof-ledger rendering and wording on Railway production.

summary: Simplified agent onboarding into a 6-step CV-first flow with draft preview, targeted gap questions, optional LinkedIn context during import, and a stronger post-onboarding trust-action handoff.
decisions: Kept onboarding server actions and validation rules intact while redesigning the page around a guided six-step sequence.
Replaced the visible full-form wall with targeted missing/low-confidence questions plus a collapsible manual editor.
Allowed onboarding to save optional achievements and languages so draft completeness feels materially stronger before the full editor.
next_steps: Run the updated onboarding and phase1 Playwright specs in an auth-enabled preview harness so they execute instead of skipping.
Do a manual desktop/mobile QA pass on /agent/onboarding and /agent/profile/edit#credibility-boost to tune spacing and copy.

summary: Added first-class structured onboarding working-style fields, switched onboarding E2E to a Supabase mock-auth harness, passed auth-enabled browser QA, and deployed the updated flow to Railway production.
decisions: Persisted fee style, transaction band, collaboration posture, and response time as first-class AgentProfile fields so they remain comparable and reusable across onboarding, profile edit, and public profile surfaces.
Reprioritized the quick interview so the seven-question budget is spent on higher-value trust and working-style signals before lower-signal manual fields.
Used a Supabase-compatible mock-auth harness for Playwright instead of the retired preview callback path, matching the app's real auth contract more closely.

Recorded production verification in two layers: successful deploy/health/auth-gate checks, and a separate authenticated live-flow blocker caused by Supabase magic-link emails still pointing at localhost.
next_steps: Correct the Supabase production magic-link redirect target so emails route to the Railway auth callback instead of localhost.
Re-run an authenticated production sanity pass through /agent/onboarding and /agent/profile/edit to confirm the live credibility-boost handoff.

summary: Hardened Supabase sign-in reliability by centralizing callback-origin resolution across public sign-in and `/auth/callback`, adding production localhost guardrails, and documenting canonical callback-origin env configuration.
decisions: Added one shared callback-origin resolver to eliminate client/server auth redirect drift across environments.
Preferred public runtime origin over localhost in production when config is misaligned, to prevent live magic-link callback failures.
Kept this change migration-free and auth-focused to restore sign-in stability quickly.
next_steps: Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_CALLBACK_ORIGIN` in Railway to the live app domain.
Align Supabase Site URL and allowed Redirect URLs with the same live callback host, then run a live magic-link sign-in sanity pass.

summary: Completed auth guardrail hardening with parallel subagent/browser verification by enforcing protected-route redirects when access-hint cookies are missing and ensuring sign-in UI pending-state recovery on thrown Supabase client errors.
decisions: Redirect authenticated-but-unresolved sessions back through `/sign-in?next=...` instead of allowing middleware pass-through on missing access hints.
Added try/catch/finally around OAuth and magic-link client actions so pending UI state cannot get stuck on runtime exceptions.
next_steps: Apply production Supabase URL configuration alignment and rerun one live magic-link sign-in sanity pass.
Update onboarding Playwright expectation text to match the latest onboarding prompt copy.

summary: Recovered public Supabase auth completion on landing/sign-in/sign-up routes, fixed the access-hint identity contract by binding it to Supabase user ids, added Google provider preflight to keep disabled providers inside WHOMA, and documented live canonical-host drift discovered through browser and curl diagnostics.
decisions: Bound `whoma_access_hint` to Supabase auth ids so middleware and `auth()` finally compare the same identity namespace.
Recovered stray public auth returns by redirecting `code` / `token_hash` / provider error params into `/auth/callback` from `/`, `/sign-in`, and `/sign-up`.
Added a server-side Google preflight route so disabled providers fail inside WHOMA with an email fallback instead of exposing raw Supabase JSON.
Preferred the active public production host for callback resolution when it differs from configured env host to reduce cross-host cookie drift.
next_steps: Choose one canonical public auth host and align Railway env, Supabase URL configuration, and magic-link templates to that same host.
Disable Google in app env until Supabase Google provider configuration is confirmed live, or enable it and rerun one successful live round-trip.
Re-run one live Google sign-in and one live magic-link sign-in on the canonical host after configuration alignment.

summary: Refreshed the root README to match the current WHOMA platform, added a durable codebase audit with scale/future-proofing recommendations, and hardened the Prisma idempotency fallback so concurrent retries reserve before executing writes.
decisions: Fixed the Prisma idempotency fallback in-session because the audit exposed a real duplicate-write risk under concurrent retries when Upstash is unavailable.
Kept the README concise and moved the deeper audit into a dedicated doc so orientation and technical follow-up stay separate.
Prioritized cached/materialized public read models and query-boundary cleanup as the next smallest safe scaling PR.
next_steps: Implement `T103` to cache or materialize public homepage and directory read models.
Introduce a stable provider-identity mapping so auth session resolution no longer relies on email-keyed request-time upserts.
Split the largest mixed-responsibility runtime files into command/query and UI/action slices.

summary: Stabilized live production auth by canonicalizing auth/app routes onto the Railway host, hiding Google while the Supabase provider remains disabled, fixing the public redirect URL to avoid `:8080`, and redeploying from a scoped release bundle.
decisions: Used the Railway host as the temporary canonical auth host because it already matched the working Supabase redirect configuration and could be repaired without depending on flaky dashboard automation.
Hid Google in production instead of leaving a broken button because the live Supabase authorize endpoint still returns an unsupported-provider error.
Deployed from a scoped temp bundle so unrelated dirty-worktree changes were not pushed live.
next_steps: Run one explicit live magic-link round-trip after confirming the destination email address.
Re-enable Google only after the Supabase provider is actually enabled and a live round-trip succeeds.
Decide whether to keep the Railway host as canonical auth or realign Supabase and Railway back to `https://www.whoma.co.uk`.

summary: Refreshed `AGENTS.md` into a senior subagent operating manual with delegation guardrails, role ownership, handoff contracts, anti-patterns, and prioritized workstreams mapped to active WHOMA roadmap items.
decisions: Added explicit senior-role ownership and a Worktree Integrator role to reduce merge risk in dirty parallel worktrees.
Translated external OpenAI agent/Codex best practices into repo-specific delegation rules instead of generic process guidance.
Anchored recommended subagent assignments to open items `T103`, `A010`, and `BV001`-`BV003` to keep orchestration outcome-driven.
next_steps: Pilot the new model on `T103` with disjoint worker ownership plus one integrator.
Require evidence bundles (changed files, validation commands, residual risks) in every subagent handoff.
Review one full delegation cycle and tighten role boundaries based on observed rework.

summary: Recovered live auth onto `https://www.whoma.co.uk`, fixed branded-host logout continuity, kept production on the verified email magic-link mode, and redeployed the scoped auth bundle until Railway served the corrected host config.
decisions: Left production on `SUPABASE_EMAIL_AUTH_METHOD=magic-link` because the Supabase hosted template could not be safely flipped to OTP in the dashboard today.
Kept Google disabled in the public UI because the live Supabase provider still fails preflight and a friendly WHOMA fallback is better than a broken outbound OAuth launch.
Redeployed the same scoped bundle twice after Railway env propagation, because the first deploy still served the earlier canonical-host behavior on `/sign-in`.
next_steps: Finish the Supabase hosted email-template switch to `{{ .Token }}` if OTP is the desired live email flow, or fully enable and verify Google sign-in instead.
Run one explicit branded-host auth round-trip on `https://www.whoma.co.uk` using a confirmed inbox address.
Update `docs/SUPABASE_AUTH_SETUP.md` so remaining auth setup guidance matches the branded-host production baseline.

summary: Clarified live passwordless auth on the branded host, prevented email sign-in from auto-creating accounts, added a resend cooldown before Supabase rate limits trip again, and redeployed the scoped auth slice to Railway production.
decisions: Kept production on `SUPABASE_EMAIL_AUTH_METHOD=magic-link` until the hosted Supabase email template can be safely switched to `{{ .Token }}`.
Kept Google hidden because the provider is still disabled in Supabase and no verified production Google OAuth credentials were available in repo or deploy config.
Deployed only the auth slice from a scoped release bundle so unrelated dirty-worktree changes stayed out of production.
next_steps: Switch the Supabase hosted email template from `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}` to `{{ .Token }}` and then change Railway production to `SUPABASE_EMAIL_AUTH_METHOD=otp`.
Run one real branded-host sign-in round-trip on `https://www.whoma.co.uk/sign-in` after the OTP switch to confirm end-to-end onboarding entry.
Create or recover the real Google OAuth client credentials, enable the Supabase Google provider, and verify one successful live Google round-trip before showing the button publicly.

summary: Merged all current branch work into `main` via fast-forward, refreshed README with team workflow guidance, and recorded the merge-completion operational deltas.
decisions: Used `--ff-only` to keep history linear because `main` could cleanly fast-forward to the prepared feature commit.
Merged all staged branch changes because the request was to merge everything into `main`.
Added README team-workflow guidance so contributors can follow the senior-subagent operating model consistently.
next_steps: Push `main` to `origin` when ready.
Begin `T103` using the updated subagent ownership and handoff protocol from `AGENTS.md`.

summary: Replaced passwordless email auth with email/password flows, fixed Google OAuth callback exchange with dashboard defaults, and restored session persistence through Supabase SSR client helpers plus middleware cookie refresh.
decisions: Introduced `/dashboard` as the default post-auth route so sign-in, sign-up, and OAuth callback paths share one deterministic landing target while preserving role/access-state routing.
Gated Google button visibility by `NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_OAUTH_ENABLED=true` to prevent broken OAuth launches in partially configured environments.
Kept canonical-host and access-hint guardrails in middleware while adding Supabase `auth.getUser()` refresh so persistence is fixed without dropping existing authorization behavior.
next_steps: Merge this auth patch to `main` and deploy to Railway production for live validation.
Run a full live auth round-trip (`sign-up`, `sign-in`, `Google OAuth`) on the branded host with callback/default routing checks.
Confirm protected-route session persistence across hard refresh and revisit.

summary: Hardened email/password signup outcome handling to stop false confirmation-success states, added inline resend-confirmation support, and updated sign-in UX to show explicit sign-in and create-account entry actions.
decisions: Used Supabase signup response semantics (`session`, `user`, `identities`) to determine post-submit UX instead of always showing a confirmation-email success message.
Added `supabase.auth.resend({ type: 'signup' })` in the same auth card to reduce support dependence when inbox delivery is delayed.
Kept existing route shells and design tokens unchanged, limiting scope to auth-flow logic and minimal copy/layout updates.
next_steps: Deploy this patch to Railway production and validate `/sign-up?role=AGENT` live.
Confirm Supabase production email-provider settings and email-confirmation posture for the active project.
Run one monitored inbox test for signup, resend, and confirmation callback routing.

summary: Hardened signup/resend confirmation redirect generation to use the canonical callback origin env value instead of browser tab origin, eliminating localhost-host leakage in production email confirmation links.
decisions: Prioritized `NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_CALLBACK_ORIGIN` for callback URL construction so production redirect links remain stable and host-canonical.
Retained runtime-origin fallback only for environments with missing or invalid callback-origin env values.
next_steps: Deploy this patch to Railway production.
Trigger a fresh signup confirmation email and verify callback host is `https://www.whoma.co.uk`.
Validate confirmation lands through `/auth/callback` and resolves to `/dashboard`.

summary: Hardened `/auth/callback` to process both Supabase email-confirmation token returns and OAuth code returns, and added regression tests for success/failure redirect behavior.
decisions: Unified email confirmation and OAuth callback session exchange in one route to reduce auth drift across environments.
Added strict supported OTP-type narrowing in the callback handler instead of using permissive casts.
next_steps: Deploy this callback hardening to Railway production.
Generate a fresh signup confirmation email and verify callback host/path resolution on `https://www.whoma.co.uk`.
Confirm post-confirm sign-in continuity on `/dashboard`.

summary: Fixed production callback redirects that were leaking to `localhost:8080` by resolving `/auth/callback` redirect origin through canonical env-backed host logic instead of raw runtime request origin.
decisions: Reused shared `resolveAuthOrigin` in callback route to keep host selection logic consistent across auth surfaces.
Added a production-mode internal-host regression test because live Railway callbacks can surface an internal localhost origin.
next_steps: Deploy this origin-resolution fix to Railway production.
Re-check `/auth/callback` headers to confirm redirect host remains on `https://www.whoma.co.uk`.
Run a fresh signup confirmation round-trip and verify immediate sign-in continuity.

summary: Added a full forgot-password flow for email/password auth: sign-in reset-email trigger, callback-safe recovery redirect, and a dedicated reset-password page to set a new password.
decisions: Placed the forgot-password action directly in the sign-in card so account recovery is discoverable without leaving the auth surface.
Routed recovery emails through `/auth/callback?next=/auth/reset-password` to preserve canonical-origin callback guarantees.
Kept password reset validation strict and inline (strength + confirm match) before `supabase.auth.updateUser`.
next_steps: Deploy this forgot-password patch to Railway production.
Run a live reset round-trip from sign-in through password update and re-authentication.
Confirm the sign-in success banner appears after reset completion (`/sign-in?reset=updated`).

summary: Restored homeowner/estate-agent role selection for authenticated users by enabling explicit switch-mode role onboarding and adding a signed-in `Switch role` header action.
decisions: Introduced explicit switch mode (`/onboarding/role?switch=1`) instead of changing default onboarding behavior for all users with existing roles.
Added role-switch entry directly in app shell for homeowner/agent sessions so role selection remains discoverable post-auth without exposing extra public-auth complexity.
next_steps: Deploy this role-switch patch to Railway production.
Validate live switch flow from homeowner to estate agent and back.
Confirm role-specific nav and route gating update correctly after each switch.

summary: Redesigned public auth UX with a premium split-shell system: multi-step no-reload sign-up with CSS transitions, role-aware Supabase signup metadata, in-flow confirmation resend, and matching sign-in layout with integrated reset-email recovery handling.
decisions: Used CSS-only stateful transitions for step choreography to satisfy the no-new-animation-library constraint.
Kept server-side callback/session guards intact while replacing auth presentation + client submit flows.
Added /auth/login alias instead of route replacement to satisfy confirmation UX path requirements without breaking existing /sign-in links.
next_steps: Run browser QA for desktop/mobile on /sign-up transition timing and confirmation resend behavior.
Validate production auth recovery round-trip on branded host (`forgot -> reset -> sign-in`).
Optionally converge remaining auth-adjacent pages onto the same split-shell layout for consistency.

summary: Locked public signup to agents only, redirected seller/homeowner public signup routes to sign-in with a coming-soon message, gated homepage metrics behind Supabase session auth, and redirected authenticated non-admin access away from the admin dashboard.
decisions: Kept seller/homeowner support in data models while enforcing public signup restrictions at UI and route-entry layers.
Added compatibility redirects for `/signup` and `/register/seller` to catch legacy entrypoints and funnel them through sign-in messaging.
Used Supabase server session detection in homepage rendering so internal metrics are hidden without deleting analytics/tracking logic.
next_steps: Install dependencies and run `npm run typecheck` plus `npm run lint` in an environment with Next.js/TypeScript binaries available.
Browser-verify `/sign-up`, `/signup?role=seller`, and `/register/seller` flows end to end.
Confirm signed-out homepage hides the metrics section and signed-in homepage renders it.

summary: Built the Past Deals verification trust loop: Supabase migration + RLS, agent add-deal flow, seller verification page, verification confirm API, and Resend seller/agent email notifications.
decisions: Kept all server-route Supabase data access on the existing SSR server client helper to match platform rules.
Used token-based public verification flow (`/verify/[token]`) with app-layer filtering and unauthenticated confirm/dispute writes.

Added `agent_name` and `agent_email` snapshots to `past_deals` so seller verification UX can show agent identity and confirmation outcomes can notify the originating agent without introducing admin-key lookups.
next_steps: Apply the Supabase migration and validate policies in the Supabase SQL editor.
Run dependency install and execute `npm run typecheck` plus `npm run lint`.
QA full flow: add deal -> seller verification email -> confirm/dispute -> agent notification email.

summary: Added a new homepage `WHOMA AGENTS` placeholder section with four mock agent profiles, responsive card grid layout, MapPin location metadata, verified-deals badge, and staggered fade-up animation.
decisions: Used a dedicated static data module (`mockAgents`) for clean replacement once live profile data is available.
Kept avatar rendering on plain `` to avoid changing Next image remote host configuration.
Added the requested Supabase replacement comment directly above the mock-data import in the homepage module.
next_steps: Swap `mockAgents` for published-agent Supabase query once production profile visibility is enabled.
Link cards to live `/agents/[slug]` records when slugs are available.

summary: Shipped support chat escalation hardening with conversation-scoped dedupe, bounded escalation payloads, tighter handoff intent detection, and runtime smoke evidence for idempotent duplicate suppression.
decisions: Used stable `conversationId` forwarding from widget to APIs to make dedupe deterministic across manual and automatic escalation paths.
Returned duplicate escalation attempts as successful idempotent responses (`success=true`, `deduped=true`) instead of errors.
Kept immediate escalation on `Talk to a person` and prevented optional email-capture confirmation from re-sending transcript content.
next_steps: Deploy rebased main and confirm live chat/escalation endpoint behavior.
Add focused route/component regression tests for escalation trigger matching and dedupe response contracts.
Rotate Anthropic and Resend keys after deployment verification.

summary: Unblocked Railway production deployment failures by removing Server Component-incompatible `styled-jsx` usage from homepage and switching the placeholder-agent animation to existing global utility classes.
decisions: Resolved deployment blocker with the smallest safe patch by reusing existing global animation utility (`animate-enter-up`) instead of introducing new client-only styling paths.
Preserved staggered card reveal behavior via `animationDelay` while keeping the page as a valid Server Component.
next_steps: Push the build-unblock patch to main and monitor Railway deployment status.
Re-run live `/api/chat` and `/api/chat/escalate` probes until expected validation responses are active.
Capture final deployment ID and smoke evidence in release notes.

summary: Resolved remaining Railway build blockers by fixing a Next.js dynamic route-handler context type and replacing a server-incompatible phosphor icon import on the homepage; local production build now succeeds with chat routes present.
decisions: Aligned dynamic route handler second-argument typing with Next.js production expectation (`params` as a Promise).
Standardized homepage icon usage on `lucide-react` to keep the route fully server-compatible during page-data collection.
next_steps: Push this final unblock patch to main and confirm Railway deployment success.
Re-run live `/api/chat` and `/api/chat/escalate` probes until expected validation responses appear.
Document final deployment ID and smoke evidence.

summary: Recovered deployability, shipped Railway production deployment d7aeddbc-6940-438d-9886-b23097ce28cb, and verified live access-control behavior with browser evidence (agent-only signup, seller-route coming-soon redirect, signed-out metrics hidden, featured agents visible, /agent/deals auth-gated).
decisions: Fixed the production build blocker by deferring Resend client creation in support-email delivery to request/send time rather than module initialization time.
Used browser-level verification for redirect outcomes because app-router behavior can differ from raw HEAD probe interpretation.
next_steps: Run one authenticated production add-deal sanity flow on /agent/deals with a controlled test account.
Run one end-to-end seller verification response cycle and confirm pending -> verified/disputed state transitions plus agent notification.
Optionally add a lightweight post-deploy smoke script for these access-control routes to prevent regressions.

summary: Added backend regression coverage for support chat escalation and hardened /api/chat payload shape validation to prevent malformed-message parser faults while preserving escalation trigger behavior.
decisions: Kept scope backend-only and did not modify UI/auth/domain modules in this pass.
Added explicit route-level tests for escalation triggers, dedupe, payload validation, and rate-limit behavior.
Tightened /api/chat message-shape validation to fail malformed payloads with 400 before transcript processing.
next_steps: Run live production smoke checks for /api/chat and /api/chat/escalate after deployment.
Move escalation dedupe state to shared storage if duplicate escalations appear across instances.

summary: Hardened support widget escalation UX, added E2E coverage, deployed Railway production release 1e6b9dde-0ba9-4666-9990-b38b9dbaa6b3, and verified live health/escalation behavior while identifying Anthropic credit exhaustion as the remaining chat-response blocker.
decisions: Moved unauthenticated escalation submission to the explicit confirm step so optional email capture can be included in the ticket context.
Removed the closed-state trigger class override that changed positioning from fixed to relative, preserving fixed-only widget behavior.
Used browser automation plus direct API probes to validate production widget and escalation behavior end-to-end.
next_steps: Restore Anthropic credits (or swap provider/model) so /api/chat returns assistant responses instead of provider credit errors.
Move escalation dedupe state to shared storage if duplicate tickets appear across instances.

summary: Fixed agent onboarding auth continuity after CV upload by compacting resume suggestion cookies, preserving sign-in next paths, preventing app error params from entering /auth/callback, restoring env-gated Google sign-in, adding mobile app navigation, and deploying Railway release 1726cf49-ea23-494d-a3da-9d1f562d898f.
decisions: Kept resume suggestions cookie-backed for this PR-sized fix but compacted payloads under a strict browser-safe cap to stop large CV drafts from destabilizing session cookies.
Preserved /sign-in error display by detecting only real Supabase callback params (code/token_hash) as callback returns.
Added mobile app navigation as a lightweight recovery path instead of introducing a larger drawer/navigation refactor.

summary: Redesigned agent onboarding into a six-step focused UI while preserving existing CV upload, resume parsing, Supabase writes, work-email verification, and onboarding completion server actions. Deployed Railway production release 625f1ecb-60af-44d7-bd6e-56a22a33d4ba, then follow-up reviewed hardening release 452feadb-4f69-46c3-8a0e-c3dd010dbd70, and verified live health.
decisions: Moved visual step sequencing into a route-local client stepper and kept the existing server page as the data/action provider.
Used browser-local step restoration because AgentProfile has no existing current_onboarding_step field and the task forbade data-model or Supabase-write changes.
Kept final completion wired to the existing submitAgentOnboardingAction rather than adding any new publish route/action behavior.
Senior review fixed client redirect-state persistence for email verification and removed invalid nested interactive controls from the import cards before the final live deployment.

next_steps: Run a real signed-in production mobile pass through CV upload, draft preview, work-email verification, and completion.
If cross-device onboarding restore is required, add schema-backed progress persistence in a separate backend PR.

summary: Added a simple file-based support knowledge base for the WHOMA chatbot, wired bounded markdown retrieval into /api/chat without changing the widget UI or base system prompt, and verified the route plus browser send path with provider-independent tests.
decisions: Kept SupportChat.tsx untouched so the existing widget UI and escalation UX remain unchanged.
Kept the base support SYSTEM_PROMPT unchanged and appends delimited knowledge context only when keyword retrieval finds relevant sections.
Used dependency-free, bounded markdown keyword retrieval instead of adding packages, embeddings, or a vector database at this MVP stage.
Switched /api/chat from edge to nodejs runtime because filesystem-backed retrieval cannot run in the Edge runtime.
verification: npm run test -- src/lib/knowledge/retrieve.test.ts src/app/api/chat/route.test.ts src/app/api/chat/escalate/route.test.ts -> passed (13/13)
