Local real-provider POST /api/chat smoke -> HTTP 200 streaming envelope with Anthropic low-credit error, confirming provider wiring and current account-credit blocker
next_steps: Keep src/content/support/*.md in sync whenever support policy, onboarding, verification, or pricing copy changes.
Restore Anthropic credits or keep provider-independent smoke tests as the deployment gate until live model streaming is available again.
If support documentation grows substantially, evaluate a build-time index or semantic retrieval in a separate PR.

summary: Redesigned /contact into a split support shell and wired POST /api/contact through the existing SupportInquiry persistence and notification service, while preserving the contact field contract and validation semantics and moving the route to a dedicated src/app/contact/page.tsx surface.
decisions: Moved /contact to a dedicated static route file and narrowed the dynamic /[slug] page back to legal/sitemap ownership to keep route responsibilities explicit.
Preserved prior contact validation semantics by reusing the same Zod constraints and category enum in the new client form before POSTing to /api/contact.
Kept the API route behind the existing SupportInquiry service so submissions remain persisted, event-tracked, rate-limited, and notified through the established lazy Resend path.
verification: Local /api/contact runtime smoke blocked in sandbox: next dev listen EPERM on 127.0.0.1:3012 (no local port binding permission)
next_steps: Submit a live /contact form in an environment with RESEND_API_KEY set and confirm inbox delivery at support@whoma.co.uk.
Optionally add a mocked integration test for /api/contact route success/error branches to lock behavior.

summary: Fixed agent post-auth routing so sign-in and signup continue through /agent/profile instead of permanently defaulting to onboarding, made completed onboarding one-time by redirecting completed agents to profile edit, and hardened onboarding CV upload file detection.
decisions: Use /agent/profile as the estate-agent portal entrypoint so it can route completed agents to profile editing and incomplete agents through onboarding.
Copy Supabase signup role metadata only when creating the local WHOMA user so future role switches are not overwritten by stale auth metadata.
Use runtime file-shape detection for CV uploads instead of instanceof File to avoid multipart runtime differences.

next_steps: Run a signed-in browser pass through create account, confirmation, /agent/profile, CV upload, draft preview, onboarding completion, and profile edit.
If product direction calls for it, add an explicit post-login portal chooser as a separate UX slice.

summary: Reviewed and hardened the current release bundle, routing /api/contact through the existing SupportInquiry persistence/service boundary, adding contact route regression coverage, normalizing touched mobile viewport shells, and rerunning full release gates.
decisions: Use the existing SupportInquiry service for public contact requests instead of a direct email-only route, preserving persistence, analytics, validation, and lazy notification behavior.
Add route-level regression tests for the public contact boundary so validation and rate-limit behavior stay locked.
Normalize touched layouts to min-h-[100dvh] to reduce mobile browser viewport issues without widening the UI scope.

summary: Hardened agent onboarding continuity by moving JS submissions through token-authenticated API paths, fixing disappearing confirmation fields, and updating onboarding question wording/categories.
decisions: Use bearer-token fallback for authenticated onboarding API calls so POST steps can survive server-action cookie read issues without weakening server authorization.
Keep existing server actions as fallback while making the JavaScript path use route handlers that return structured errors instead of redirecting unexpectedly to sign-in.
Freeze the confirmation-field list per draft so fields stay visible and editable while typing.
Keep the existing transactionBand storage name for compatibility while presenting user-facing property category options.
Treat response time as self-reported for now and document the need for measured behaviour-derived metrics later.

summary: Moved the active Past Deals add/verification path to Prisma/Postgres, updated the role vocabulary, removed Referral from validation/UI, and made seller-email delivery failures non-blocking after a deal is saved.
decisions: Use Railway-managed Prisma/Postgres for the active PastDeal write path so deployment applies the required schema automatically.
Keep the seller-verification email as a follow-up side effect: if the deal is saved but delivery fails, return a successful response with a warning instead of blocking the user.
Represent the requested roles as sole_agent, multi_agent, and buyers_agent across UI, validation, API, and persistence.
